● SCOTS
Referral Appointment After Care Locations
Referral Appointment After Care Locations

What to expect from your appointment

This page explains why you have been referred to SCOTS, what happens during your appointment, and how to prepare.

Why have I been referred?

You have been referred to SCOTS to assess your musculoskeletal condition. This means problems with your joints, muscles, or soft tissues. We will work out the best next step for your care.

This may include:

  • Advice and education
  • A structured rehabilitation plan
  • An education program
  • Referral for X-rays or scans (if needed)
  • Referral to a bone and joint specialist

Will I see a surgeon?

No, not at this stage.

Your first appointment is with a Physiotherapist. They are trained to work out whether:

  • Your condition can improve without surgery, or
  • You would benefit from seeing a surgeon

This makes sure you get the right care at the right time.

Why not go straight to a surgeon?

For many conditions:

  • Surgery is not the first or best treatment
  • Pain often improves with the right non-surgical care
  • Seeing a surgeon too early may not change your outcome

Our job is to make sure that if you do need surgery, the referral is appropriate and likely to help you.

What will happen at my appointment?

At your appointment, we will:

  1. 1 Listen to your concerns and goals
  2. 2 Assess your condition
  3. 3 Review any previous treatment
  4. 4 Discuss your options together

Together, we will agree on a clear plan forward.

What if I think I need surgery?

We understand you may be hoping for a surgical opinion. During your appointment, we will explain whether surgery is likely to help you.

If surgery is right for you, we will refer you. If not, we will explain the best next steps and why.

Surgery is not ruled out — it is carefully considered at the right time.

What if I have already tried physiotherapy?

That is helpful information. We will look at:

  • What type of treatment you had
  • How long you tried it for
  • Whether it was the right kind for your problem

Sometimes a different approach can still help.

What happens after the assessment?

Depending on your results, we may:

  • Start or adjust a rehabilitation plan
  • Invite you to an education program
  • Discharge you with advice
  • Refer you for X-rays or scans
  • Refer you for a surgical opinion

If treatment is recommended, we will set a clear timeframe and check on your progress.

How to prepare for your appointment

A Bring any scan reports or letters
B Wear comfortable clothing
C Think about how your condition affects daily life

If you have questions, speak with your GP or the person who referred you.
